title: What is the difference between citizens and non-citizens: level 1
description: Test your knowledge on the difference between the rights of citizens and non-citizens!
topic_path: What is the difference between citizens and non-citizens?
What is the difference between citizens and non-citizens: level 1

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following phrases best defines the principle of *jus soli*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: any child born in a country is a citizen of that country
any person who has one or more parents who are citizens inherits their citizenship
any person born in a foreign country earns citizenship through naturalization
any child of parents with multiple citizenships must choose one

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following phrases best defines *jus sanguinis*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: any person who has one or more parents who are citizens inherits their citizenship
any child born in a country is a citizen of that country
any person born in a foreign country earns citizenship through naturalization
any child of parents with multiple citizenship must choose one

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines refugees?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a person who lives in a country outside of their nationality out of fear of persecution and/or fear for their life
a person who has relocated to another country to live there permanently
a person who has moved to another country without getting the proper authorization from that country’s immigration authorities
a person who is a legally recognized member of a state

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines nationals?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a person under the protection of a nation without having the formal status of a citizen
a person who has moved to another country without getting the proper authorization from that country’s immigration authorities
a person who meets the definition of a refugee and is seeking admission into a country at a port of entry
a person who lives in a country outside of their nationality out of fear of persecution and/or fear for their life

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines dual national citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the status in which a person is a national of two or more countries at the same time
the process a country goes through to revoke one’s allegiance to one’s native country
the right to vote as given by one’s government
the principle in which the citizenship status of one’s parent determines one’s status

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines a dual national?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a person allegiant to two or more countries at the same time
a person who is a legally recognized member of a state
a person who has relocated to another country to live there permanently
a person who inherited their citizenship status from their parents

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best defines an asylum seeker?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a person who meets the definition of a refugee and is seeking admission into a country at a port of entry
a person who has citizenship in and is loyal to two or more countries at the same time
a person who is under the protection of a nation without having the formal status of a citizen
a person who has left their home in fear of their safety, but they have remained in their country’s borders

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes the goal of Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to postpone the deportation of young, undocumented immigrants brought to the United States by their parents
to identify and remove undocumented immigrants who have been living in the United States
to create a pathway to citizenship for young, undocumented immigrants brought by their parents
to remove barriers to higher education like lower tuition costs for undocumented immigrants

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes one advantage a citizen has by having dual citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They are eligible for important benefits from the other country or countries they are loyal to.
They no longer have to uphold the responsibilities or meet the obligations of American citizenship.
They can move to other countries freely without having to get permission from the United States government.
They can use their dual nationality to hold elected office in either country’s government.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes one argument against a citizen having dual citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It may cause conflicting loyalties if a citizen is a dual citizen, especially if the country is hostile to the United States.
It limits the United States government’s ability to collect taxes because they cannot tax people who are citizens of another country.
It gives dual national citizens benefits that other Americans do not have because they are not dual citizens.
It prohibits dual nationals from being able to vote in federal elections because the government considers them a foreign influence.

assessment_items.question_content:
| DREAM Act | DACA program
- | - | -
**A** | has loose eligibility requirements and applies to millions of immigrants | has strict eligibility requirements, including an age limit
**B** | applies to all undocumented immigrants | applies to undocumented immigrants brought to the United States as children
**C** | permits undocumented immigrants to apply for Social Security and Medicare | allows undocumented immigrants to apply for lawful permanent status
**D**| creates a path to citizenship for eligible undocumented immigrants | de-prioritizes the deportation of young, law-abiding undocumented immigrants

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares the DREAM Act to the DACA program?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| Lawful permanent resident | Citizen
- | - | -
**A** | obligated to register with the selective service if they meet the eligible criteria | obligated to pay federal and state taxes
**B** | can work in any job they are qualified for | can only work in jobs that the government has approved 
**C** | must live in certain designated cities with approval from USCIS | can live anywhere in the United States without getting permission from the government
**D**| required to obey international laws, but not US laws | required to obey all laws of the United States, states, and localities

**Which of the following pairings accurately compares the rights and responsibilities of a lawful permanent resident and a citizen ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| Refugee | Lawful permanent resident
- | - | -
**A** | are automatically considered citizens of the United States | do not have a path to naturalization
**B** | attend public school according to state laws | cannot apply for a job requiring US citizenship
**C** | move to another country out of fear of persecution in their home country  | move to the United States for any number of reasons, including job opportunities
**D**| has all the rights granted under the Bill of Rights | protected by US law, but not protected under the Bill of Rights

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| Lawful permanent resident | Undocumented immigrant
- | - | -
**A** |can only live in areas approved by USCIS| can live and work anywhere in the United States where they can get work
**B** | entered the United States with the proper documentation and approval | entered the United States without authorization or stayed beyond their visa 
**C** | can go through the naturalization process after five years | can go through the naturalization process if they were brought here as children
**D**| can vote in federal elections | cannot hold any position in the federal government

Which comparison best represents the definitions of lawful permanent resident and undocumented immigrant?

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following groups of people get citizenship at birth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: children born on United States soil
children who have one parent who is an American citizen
children of foreign ambassadors to the United States
children of refugees to the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are rights afforded to citizens with dual nationality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: freedom to choose your employment
right to vote in federal elections
right to attend public school
freedom to travel without a passport

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are benefits that Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) recipients get for being in the program?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: allowed to live in the United States without fear of deportation
authorized to work in the United States
permitted to apply for and receive healthcare benefits
become lawful permanent residents and can apply for naturalization

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following groups of people are eligible to apply for  Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: people who entered the United States when they were fifteen years old or younger
people who have not committed a serious felony or significant misdemeanor
relatives of a DACA recipient who has proven to be a productive member of society
relatives of citizens who have successfully completed the naturalization process

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are reasons a refugee would get permission to relocate to the United States?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: fear of persecution based on their race, religion, or nationality
fear of death because of increased violence in their home country
fear of losing state-provided benefits because of an economic crisis
fear of their children not being able to advance in life because of poor schooling

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are rights that lawful permanent residents have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to live and work anywhere in the country
right apply for Social Security and Medicare benefits
right to protected free speech and to worship as they choose
right to run for an elected federal position like president

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following are responsibilities and obligations that refugees have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: obey all federal, state, and local laws
pay federal, state, and local taxes
send their adult children to college
serve in the American military
